qa_pairs.question: What is required if a multi-year Research Platforms and Portals (RPP) award expires in 2026 and resources are still needed?
qa_pairs.answer: If your multi-year RPP award expires in 2026, you must submit a new RPP application this fall if your need for resources will continue for the 2026-2027 allocation cycle.

qa_pairs.question: What is the maximum duration for multi-year Research Platforms and Portals (RPP) allocations?
qa_pairs.answer: Allocations for RPP projects may be awarded over multiple years, with a maximum of three years, subject to an annual review and availability of resources.

qa_pairs.question: Do awarded multi-year RPP projects need to submit a new application every year?
qa_pairs.answer: No, awarded multi-year RPP projects do not need to submit a new application every year, but they are required to complete an annual progress report.

qa_pairs.question: How will a Principal Investigator (PI) be informed about the RPP Annual Progress Report process?
qa_pairs.answer: The PI will be notified by email with instructions for the information required and the submission process.

qa_pairs.question: When are the submissions for the annual RPP Progress Report due in 2025?
qa_pairs.answer: Submissions of the annual RPP Progress Report start on September 23 and are due on November 4, 2025.

qa_pairs.question: What are the eligibility requirements for a Principal Investigator (PI) and Co-PIs to submit a Resource Allocation Competition (RAC) application?
qa_pairs.answer: To be eligible, the PI and all Co-PIs must be a faculty member at a Canadian academic institution and have an active Alliance account in the CCDB with an Academic PI role (Faculty, Adjunct Faculty, or Librarian).

qa_pairs.question: How many Resources for Research Groups (RRG) applications can a Principal Investigator (PI) submit at one time?
qa_pairs.answer: An eligible PI can apply as PI for only one RRG application at a time (either through the full application or the Fast Track process), but can be involved in multiple RRG submissions as a co-PI or a collaborator.

qa_pairs.question: How many Research Platforms and Portals (RPP) applications can a Principal Investigator (PI) lead?
qa_pairs.answer: An eligible PI can be the PI for one RPP application per competition round and be involved in other RPP applications as a co-PI.

qa_pairs.question: What should a Principal Investigator (PI) ensure regarding their position before submitting a RAC application?
qa_pairs.answer: An eligible PI must apply with their primary, most up-to-date position. If the PI recently moved to a different institution or has more than one active faculty role, they must ensure their new or most up-to-date position is set as the primary role in the CCDB portal.

qa_pairs.question: What is the consequence of not setting the most up-to-date position as primary for a Principal Investigator (PI)?
qa_pairs.answer: Failing to do so may cause problems if your application is successful.

qa_pairs.question: Are 'departmental' applications accepted for the Resource Allocation Competition (RAC)?
qa_pairs.answer: No, 'departmental' applications, defined as applications submitted on behalf of a group of PIs not collaborating in a common research project, will not be accepted.

qa_pairs.question: What is the definition of a Co-PI in the context of this competition?
qa_pairs.answer: A Co-PI is any Canadian faculty with an Alliance Academic PI role that is actively involved in the computational project.

qa_pairs.question: What documentation is required from the Principal Investigator (PI) and Co-PIs for a RAC application?
qa_pairs.answer: The PI and any Co-PI must upload an updated Canadian Common CV (CCV) with the application.

qa_pairs.question: What must be done regarding the role and involvement of any Co-PI in a RAC application?
qa_pairs.answer: The role and involvement of any Co-PI listed in the application must be justified.

qa_pairs.question: Who can be listed as a Collaborator in a project?
qa_pairs.answer: A Collaborator is any other person actively participating in the project who cannot be listed as a Co-PI based on the provided definition, such as international investigators or colleagues without an Alliance account.

qa_pairs.question: Where should Collaborators be listed in the application document?
qa_pairs.answer: International investigators or colleagues without an Alliance account can be listed as collaborators in the Resource Management section of the application document.

qa_pairs.question: When should a researcher not submit a Resource Allocation Competition (RAC) application?
qa_pairs.answer: If your need for computational resources can be met with what will be available via the Rapid Access Service (RAS), do not submit a RAC application.

qa_pairs.question: Which types of storage are not allocated through the Resource Allocation Competition (RAC)?
qa_pairs.answer: /HOME storage and /SCRATCH storage are not allocated through the RAC.

qa_pairs.question: What is the minimum CPU request for HPC resources to be eligible for a RAC application?
qa_pairs.answer: A CPU request must be equal to or greater than 200 core-years or core-equivalent-years in each of the clusters where these resources are needed.

qa_pairs.question: How do you calculate core-equivalent-years for a CPU request that is less than 200 core-years but requires high memory per core?
qa_pairs.answer: Use the formula: core-equivalent-years = MAX(cores, mem requested / 4GB), where Mem requested = CY * mem per core requested. The resulting calculation must be greater than 200 for the request to be eligible for a RAC application.
